content: Autonomous curiosity mechanisms inherent to curious reinforcement learning agents can be repurposed to dynamically prune and prioritize long-term memory storage for state-of-the-art 2024-2025 active learning agents, solving the critical problem of unbounded memory bloat that plagues modern generalist agents. This curiosity-driven memory gating system will only retain episodic memories that trigger high intrinsic reward from unresolvable uncertainty, automatically discarding low-information redundant content without manual tuning.
